Big spacious newly refurbished toilets.. The building is old and has no elevator, but it is just 3 flights of stairs so hardly matters. They supply RO water, they have a kitchen which serves decent food At a reasonable price. Check all your apps for a better price on the rooms.. For the price I paid, this room was a steal.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"sai mukthevi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXiw4ruM-veKZv6QOKlmK7mUx5imdY2qaCOkiIZh7PiXm7FeRh0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Overall Experience: Extremely Disappointing
My stay at Hotel Airport Inn was nothing short of a terrible experience. The reality of the hotel is vastly different from what is advertised online, with the actual conditions being far worse than expected.

Room Quality & Cleanliness: Below Standard
The rooms had a persistent, unpleasant odor and were far from clean. The walls had visible cracks, giving the place an abandoned feel. The mattresses were unclean, making it difficult to feel comfortable.
The bathroom was in an unhygienic state upon check-in and was only cleaned after I raised a complaint.
Several basic amenities were missing, including a dental kit, soap, and even a dustbin.
The TV and AC were non-functional, adding to the discomfort.
The bed’s headboard was covered in dust, and to make matters worse, leftover food from previous guests was still in the room, showcasing a severe lack of housekeeping standards.
Staff Behavior & Service: Average but Lacking Responsibility
While the staff was polite, they failed to take proactive responsibility for the poor condition of the rooms. The management should have informed guests about the state of the hotel beforehand, rather than letting them check in under false expectations.

Food & Dining Experience: Not Applicable
We chose not to dine at the hotel, so I cannot comment on the food quality.

Amenities (Gym, Pool, Wi-Fi, etc.): Almost Non-Existent
We had no expectations for additional amenities, and rightly so, as none were provided. However, the Wi-Fi was functional, which was the only positive aspect.

Unique Aspect of the Hotel: Unanimously Bad Condition
If there is anything "unique" about this hotel, it is the consistent dissatisfaction among guests. Many others at the property had similar complaints, indicating that this is not an isolated issue but rather a recurring problem.

Issues & Problems Faced:
Since we had prepaid for our booking, we had no option but to check in despite the poor conditions, as refunds were not allowed.
Due to the unbearable state of the room, we had to pay extra to upgrade to a slightly better, but still subpar, room.
If renovation or maintenance is ongoing, the hotel should not have accepted bookings, as guests should not be forced into such an uncomfortable experience, especially in a metro city like Delhi.
Would I Recommend This Hotel?
Absolutely not. This hotel does not meet even the most basic hospitality standards. If you're looking for a comfortable stay, I strongly advise choosing a different hotel.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCQz7vQ-wE&fid=0x0:0x44d249bcf6e57001"],"When":"2025-3-8"}]

[{"Name":"Yogesh Thakur","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWLYTboL77pRf_LT0gIcNi0STps7BRQie5001JE5adPwkT7K1M=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"During a recent visit to Delhi, we stayed at this hotel for two nights and had a wonderful experience. The property is relatively new, and the rooms were spacious, clean, and very comfortable. The bed and linens were excellent, which made for a restful stay.

The reception team was welcoming, and the overall staff was courteous and helpful. However, the highlight of our stay was truly the culinary team. My daughter has multiple food allergies and must be extremely careful with her meals. I contacted the hotel’s Executive Chef, Deep, a few days before our arrival, and he thoughtfully connected me with Chef Vinay, who was on duty during our stay.

Chef Vinay went above and beyond to ensure my daughter’s safety and comfort. He prepared her meals from scratch, took special care to prevent any cross-contamination, and made sure everything she ate was safe. Not only was the food safe, but it was also delicious—my daughter was genuinely happy with both the taste and the kindness she received from Chef Vinay and his team.

They even arranged packaged breakfasts for us on the day of our early departure and prepared takeaway coffee, which was a thoughtful touch we truly appreciated.

We are extremely pleased with our experience and would gladly recommend this hotel to anyone. We look forward to staying here again on our next visit.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Christoph","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKJ-xhSUjNvvIdciKGKPx777M1bKnyY7vYDy-0DetwPOTzFr4I=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I had the pleasure of staying at this hotel for four weeks and truly enjoyed my time there. Rooms were neat too. Hotel staff were accommodating. Only downside would be the room service food which was technically prepared outside the hotel in a small space right beside the main entrance which I found to be a lil odd and not the cleanest since the kitchen is right off of the road. Otherwise pretty good.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Shahanawaz Shaikh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW0kPDBnC09ms8XNnumQ9qjAujcmuGPHyd0xS_qAPtlNBw31rsF=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I would not recommend this hotel based on my experience.

Hotel to Hospital journey

The check-in process was smooth and the room itself was decent. Room service staff were polite and responsive. However, there are serious shortcomings that overshadow these positives.

The hotel provides hot water only in the morning between 6 am and 12 pm. There is no hot water available at night, which is extremely inconvenient, especially during winters. This limitation was not clearly communicated at the time of booking.

The biggest disappointment was the restaurant. They advertise a rooftop restaurant, but it exists only in name. Service was extremely slow and unprofessional. Orders took anywhere between 45 minutes to one hour, with no accountability or clear communication from the staff. We were so frustrated that we decided to leave and eat outside, but the staff convinced us to stay and assured quick service.

Eventually, food was served after another long wait, but the quality was extremely poor. Every family member faced severe digestion issues, including vomiting. The situation was serious enough that we had to rush to the hospital the very next day. This was a disturbing and unacceptable experience.

We informed the reception about the food quality and the health issues caused. While they assured us that action would be taken, no one ever followed up or contacted us again.

Overall, this stay caused stress, health issues, and disappointment. Due to poor restaurant hygiene, lack of accountability, and misleading claims, I would strongly advise others to avoid this hotel.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kajal Khirodkar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVp4yLhXsSxz5FRhLIs5ELy9xLpSju5KM3SkRStt5UaJNgp2-4y9Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Booked this hotel for family trip. The rooms were good and well maintained. There were no windows in the room but still rooms were very well ventilated. The room service was good they cleaned the rooms almost daily and food was also good. There is a roof top restaurant we mostly had dinner there as it had cozy garden roof top ambience. My stay here was comfortable. Definitely recommend this hotel if someone wants to stay near the new Delhi railway station area.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Hari Prasad Kanakkan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVSrMxNSQZag06bQ1E148d6vLTQJzbTyG3llinHNMh-IcTOGnA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"If you already know this location, you might be able to manage staying here — but if you’re a first-timer in Delhi, never choose this place.

It’s located in the middle of a very crowded local road. If you stand even for a second on or near the roadside, there is a 200% chance a bike or rickshaw will hit you. There are countless two-wheelers, people walking, running, cooking, eating, selling, buying — everything happens on the road itself. In the photos, the hotel looks like a five-star facility, but the moment you enter, you’ll realise it’s a complete mess.

If you’re a foreigner who wants to experience the “raw” side of India, this might be the place for you. But if you’re someone visiting Delhi for the first time from outside the city, this experience might make you never want to return.

Now, about the rooms: the booking price is around ₹2,000–₹3,000 and advertised as a “premium deluxe AC stay,” but the rooms are actually very basic and worth maybe ₹1,000 at best. The rooms are old, not soundproof at all, and you can hear constant honking, as well as staff laughing and talking throughout the night. They claim the kitchen is open at all times, but the options are very limited. No black tea or coffee during breakfast, and the breakfast itself is just for namesake — nothing proper.

All this could still be tolerated, but the front desk staff is not friendly at all, especially the older man who comes in the evening and the lady at the desk. They never smile — unless you’re a foreigner. The older man always talks in Hindi, and often in a rude tone, as if we are staying there for free.

I will never choose this place again, and I strongly advise non-Delhi natives not to stay here. You will end up hating this stay — and possibly the city itself.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDCB1-hAlnbSz8OnZlTYzuo&fid=0x0:0xc56eca301aae5153"],"When":"2025-11-25"},{"Name":"Masab Khalid","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWQIbHlGIm9edYog9zr0Yx1KJEsjt8RdeJ3wpXom_pC4jeOK4HE=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"We wanted to post an honest review on booking.com, and we got the notification that a review has already been posted from us, which gave it 10 stars. It was not at all 10 star experience, it was actually quite a bad experience, we couldn’t sleep the whole night because there was a wedding happening right next to it which was playing loud music throughout the night. A special mention goes to the housekeeping team, especially Mukesh, for maintaining impeccable cleanliness and ensuring every comfort was taken care of. His friendly attitude made a real difference throughout our stay.
All staff members, including the security team, were consistently polite and welcoming—always greeting us with a smile. The breakfast spread was fresh, varied, and satisfying, and the kids really enjoyed the play area.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Mohit Sarin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKpcqCMl4L3L_zvyF_qf5d8DGgA3P-jabYrvmx_8R2Tr1yLeAq0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"My stay at IIDL Suites in Mayur Vihar, New Delhi, was marred by a serious invasion of privacy that left me feeling unsafe and disrespected. Although I had deliberately opted out of housekeeping service (no "Clean my room" request selected, and no need for it that day), a male housekeeping staff member entered my occupied room uninvited and without proper announcement.

I caught him in the act inside the room and immediately asked him to leave. His lame excuse? He claimed he rang the doorbell three times, got no answer, and decided he had the "right" to let himself in anyway—for housekeeping that I hadn't even requested! This isn't just poor training; it's a blatant disregard for basic guest privacy and boundaries. No waiting for permission and just assuming entry is fine because the guest didn't respond. In what world does that make sense for a supposed "luxury" serviced apartment?

When I reported this disturbing incident to the front desk receptionist, their response was infuriating: they shrugged it off as "routine" practice. No apology, no concern for my discomfort, no assurance that it wouldn't happen again or that the staff would be corrected. Instead, they defended the behavior as normal! It's shocking that the management at IIDL Suites allows—and even normalizes—such intrusions, treating a paying guest's explicit preference for privacy as "optional".

Shame on the management for cultivating this cavalier attitude toward guest safety and respect. In any professional hotel, especially one rebranded from Fraser Suites and trying to position itself as upscale, staff should never enter an occupied room without clear permission, particularly when service hasn't been requested. A simple acknowledgment of the mistake and an apology could have salvaged the situation, but their indifference speaks volumes about their priorities: apparently, "routine" violations trump customer comfort.

This property's history of rebranding (from Fraser Suites to IIDL) suggests ongoing issues, and if they continue tolerating this kind of incompetent, boundary-ignoring staff without accountability, it's no wonder they struggle to maintain a strong reputation.

If you prioritize your personal privacy, feeling secure in your room, and being treated with basic courtesy, strongly avoid IIDL Suites. There are far better hotels in Delhi that actually understand hospitality and respect guest requests. Save yourself the frustration and book elsewhere!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Aman Kumar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJeYawtmtg4oGlGsgOq2ermjEK-id6wZWWEnm3gEHEr6TOyag=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Had a wonderful experience staying at this hotel. We immediately complained to housekeeping. The housekeeping manager, Ankit Singhan, came along with an associate. Instead of showing concern or apologizing, they were smiling and behaving very casually, which made the situation even more disturbing. There was absolutely no sense of responsibility, professionalism, or basic courtesy toward a guest who had just faced such a disgusting experience.

The hotel completely failed to meet even the most basic standards of cleanliness, hygiene, and hospitality.
To begin with, the bedding condition was disgusting. The blanket provided was torn, the bedsheet was dusty, and the bed liner was filthy, covered with visible stains and spots. After sleeping on this bed, we developed rashes, which clearly shows how unhygienic and unsafe the bedding was. This is absolutely unacceptable and poses a serious health concern.
The room facilities were another major letdown. The hotel website clearly mentions that rooms are equipped with a kettle and fridge, but neither was available in our room. This is false advertising and misleading for guests. The handwash bottle in the washroom was dirty and sticky, making us question whether the bathroom had been cleaned at all.
Basic amenities were not provided proactively. No drinking water was kept in the room, and we had to ask for it explicitly, which should never be the case in any hotel. Even after repeated disappointments, the service continued to get worse. The TV connection was cut off before our checkout time, which is extremely unprofessional and inconvenient for guests who are still staying in the room.
The behavior of the receptionist was equally disappointing. Instead of being polite or helpful, the receptionist was rude, uncooperative, and lacked basic courtesy. There was no apology or concern shown when we raised issues, which reflects very poor customer service and management attitude.
Overall, this hotel shows complete disregard for guest comfort, hygiene, and honest service. It is not worth the money, time, or stress. I would strongly advise travelers to avoid this place unless they want to risk their health and deal with extremely poor service. Only downside would be the room service food which was technically prepared outside the hotel in a small space right beside the main entrance which I found to be a lil odd and not the cleanest since the kitchen is right off of the road. Otherwise pretty good.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Shahanawaz Shaikh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW0kPDBnC09ms8XNnumQ9qjAujcmuGPHyd0xS_qAPtlNBw31rsF=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I would not recommend this hotel based on my experience.

Hotel to Hospital journey

The check-in process was smooth and the room itself was decent. Room service staff were polite and responsive. However, there are serious shortcomings that overshadow these positives.

The hotel provides hot water only in the morning between 6 am and 12 pm. There is no hot water available at night, which is extremely inconvenient, especially during winters. This limitation was not clearly communicated at the time of booking.

The biggest disappointment was the restaurant. They advertise a rooftop restaurant, but it exists only in name. Service was extremely slow and unprofessional. Orders took anywhere between 45 minutes to one hour, with no accountability or clear communication from the staff. We were so frustrated that we decided to leave and eat outside, but the staff convinced us to stay and assured quick service.

Eventually, food was served after another long wait, but the quality was extremely poor. Every family member faced severe digestion issues, including vomiting. The situation was serious enough that we had to rush to the hospital the very next day. This was a disturbing and unacceptable experience.

We informed the reception about the food quality and the health issues caused. While they assured us that action would be taken, no one ever followed up or contacted us again.

Overall, this stay caused stress, health issues, and disappointment. Due to poor restaurant hygiene, lack of accountability, and misleading claims, I would strongly advise others to avoid this hotel.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kajal Khirodkar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVp4yLhXsSxz5FRhLIs5ELy9xLpSju5KM3SkRStt5UaJNgp2-4y9Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Booked this hotel for family trip. The rooms were good and well maintained. There were no windows in the room but still rooms were very well ventilated. The room service was good they cleaned the rooms almost daily and food was also good. There is a roof top restaurant we mostly had dinner there as it had cozy garden roof top ambience. My stay here was comfortable. Definitely recommend this hotel if someone wants to stay near the new Delhi railway station area.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Hari Prasad Kanakkan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVSrMxNSQZag06bQ1E148d6vLTQJzbTyG3llinHNMh-IcTOGnA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"If you already know this location, you might be able to manage staying here — but if you’re a first-timer in Delhi, never choose this place.

It’s located in the middle of a very crowded local road. If you stand even for a second on or near the roadside, there is a 200% chance a bike or rickshaw will hit you. There are countless two-wheelers, people walking, running, cooking, eating, selling, buying — everything happens on the road itself. In the photos, the hotel looks like a five-star facility, but the moment you enter, you’ll realise it’s a complete mess.

If you’re a foreigner who wants to experience the “raw” side of India, this might be the place for you. But if you’re someone visiting Delhi for the first time from outside the city, this experience might make you never want to return.

Now, about the rooms: the booking price is around ₹2,000–₹3,000 and advertised as a “premium deluxe AC stay,” but the rooms are actually very basic and worth maybe ₹1,000 at best. The rooms are old, not soundproof at all, and you can hear constant honking, as well as staff laughing and talking throughout the night. They claim the kitchen is open at all times, but the options are very limited. No black tea or coffee during breakfast, and the breakfast itself is just for namesake — nothing proper.

All this could still be tolerated, but the front desk staff is not friendly at all, especially the older man who comes in the evening and the lady at the desk. They never smile — unless you’re a foreigner. The older man always talks in Hindi, and often in a rude tone, as if we are staying there for free.

I will never choose this place again, and I strongly advise non-Delhi natives not to stay here. You will end up hating this stay — and possibly the city itself.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDCB1-hAlnbSz8OnZlTYzuo&fid=0x0:0xc56eca301aae5153"],"When":"2025-11-25"},{"Name":"Masab Khalid","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWQIbHlGIm9edYog9zr0Yx1KJEsjt8RdeJ3wpXom_pC4jeOK4HE=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"We wanted to post an honest review on booking.com, and we got the notification that a review has already been posted from us, which gave it 10 stars. It was not at all 10 star experience, it was actually quite a bad experience, we couldn’t sleep the whole night because there was a wedding happening right next to it which was playing loud music throughout the night. Bathrooms were not very clean, room temperature was quite chilly.
The staff was nice though, they were always available for whatever help we wanted, except the music situation of course.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Jithin Vijayan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWgsRpawpYwdztuey_EUUpuykNl4g8tbEaGYE_X7fLX26pky74r=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I had a great stay here. The hotel is located very close to the airport, which makes it extremely convenient for early flights or transit stays.

Rooms:
The rooms were clean, comfortable, and better maintained compared to many nearby properties. The interiors felt modern, tidy, and well-organised. The bed was comfortable, and overall room quality was impressive for the price.

Cleanliness:
This was one of the biggest positives. The room, washroom, and linens were all neat and fresh. The hotel clearly pays attention to cleanliness, which made the stay comfortable.

Service:
The staff were polite, quick to assist, and always helpful — from check-in to room service. They responded promptly when needed and made the stay smooth.

Food:
The in-room dining was decent and served on time. Breakfast options were simple but good enough for a short stay.

Location:

title: Hotel Le Seasons
address: 8 A, Hotel Le Seasons, Metro Station, N.H, Rd Number 1, opposite Aerocity, A Block, Block B, Mahipalpur Extension, Mahipalpur, New Delhi, Delhi 110037

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Amrit Chatterjee","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXX-eYJZXkYHiHIPZ9Ef-hqWeIysNXS7bd8sK9InCPlok8Y4V3n=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"My recent stay at Hotel Le Season was primarily for an overnight short official transit, and for that purpose, it’s an excellent, strategically located choice. The major selling point is its incredible proximity to New Delhi International Airport and its direct location on the NH-8 service road, which makes getting to or from the terminals a quick and hassle-free affair.
The Deluxe Room I occupied was very clean, well-maintained, and surprisingly soundproof—a huge plus given the busy road outside—it felt great and the decor leaned toward the traditional side, suggesting the property is well maintained. The air conditioning worked perfectly, but some of the bathroom fittings could use a modern update.
Service, however, was generally a highlight. The front desk staff were quick, courteous, and efficient with the express check-in and check-out process. They were clearly accustomed to handling all the guests/ travelers.
The on-site dining as well as breakfast is decent. I found the quality of the Indian dishes at the breakfast to be quite flavorful and sufficient variety for continental options as well. Be aware that dining in the hotel can be a little pricey compared to local restaurants.
Overall, if you are looking for an extremely convenient, clean, and reliably comfortable place to crash for an airport layover or a short business trip where proximity to Aerocity and the Metro is key, Le Season delivers. It’s certainly a better value than some of the more expensive Aerocity properties, provided you are willing to overlook the somewhat aging interiors.
Pros: Unbeatable airport proximity, excellent soundproofing, fast check-in.
Cons: Rooms can be small, internal food prices are high.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Vansh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU_jEZc-dTzn4N3seNodmbD7Fo7NVowd-dDGlhsXRJyDbdBEupjcg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I had a wonderful stay at hotel le seasons— the staff were warm, welcoming, and always ready to help, which made me feel truly cared for throughout my visit. The rooms were spotless, well-maintained, and offered a comfortable space to relax after sightseeing. The food, especially the breakfast buffet, was delicious with plenty of variety to suit every taste. What stood out the most was the calm and peaceful ambiance of the property, with beautiful interiors and thoughtful amenities that added to the overall experience. The hotel’s location was also very convenient, making it easy to explore the nearby attractions. Overall, it was a delightful experience and I would happily return. I would also like to show my gratitude to Mr Dhirendra for letting me know about his workplace....","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Prakash Rao","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWo_h3MjU9Uk68u7QqQcs68HdxjK21I7iLMNyLY4NAuxia-S3-G=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A hotel very near to airport. My husband and I stayed at this hotel for two nights during our visit to Delhi, India. On arrival, we were given a very small room with a full-size bed, and we didn’t sleep well the first night. The next morning, I requested a room change, and the staff kindly moved us to a larger room with a king bed. Unfortunately, that room had quite a few tiny black flies, and the TV didn’t work. Although I asked for it to be fixed, nothing was done except that they took the remote.

The bathroom was fine and had consistent hot water. The bed was clean, and the Wi-Fi worked well throughout our stay, which we really appreciated. Our room included breakfast — the buffet was okay, though limited for foreigners who prefer non-spicy options like my husband. There were boiled eggs and cereal available, which helped.

We also ate dinner at the hotel’s restaurant; the food was tasty but quite spicy. Overall, it’s a decent option if you’re on a budget and just need a clean place to rest for the night.
